Policy claims use current official USC policy, academic integrity, and ITS pages; news, course-template, login-required, and inaccessible results were rejected.

artifacts.claimText: USC’s Generative AI General Policy says Internal Use Only, Confidential, and Restricted Confidential Data may not be entered into Individual AI Tools, and Restricted Confidential Data may not be entered into any AI Tool, including USC Enterprise AI Tools, unless the Office of Ethics and Compliance gives written approval.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI General Policy - Policies and Policy Governance
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: USC’s Generative AI General Policy states that covered individuals are responsible for checking generative AI outputs for accuracy and completeness and are responsible for AI-generated output used in USC work product.

citation.citationTitle: Generative AI General Policy - Policies and Policy Governance
citation.sourceRights: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.

artifacts.claimText: USC’s Generative AI General Policy says departments, schools, and units may not acquire Individual AI Tools without first consulting the Office of Cybersecurity, Office of Ethics and Compliance, and Office of the General Counsel.

artifacts.claimText: USC’s Office of Academic Integrity says students are expected to confirm with instructors what generative AI use, if any, is permitted in coursework before using it, and that ChatGPT Edu access does not change course policy expectations.

artifacts.claimText: USC’s Office of Academic Integrity states that work authored by another, including material created by ChatGPT or other generative AI tools, is considered plagiarism when represented as the student’s own work.

artifacts.title: University of Southern California staged AI policy crawl draft
artifacts.summary: Staged draft based on official USC sources: a university-wide Generative AI General Policy, Office of Academic Integrity guidance for coursework and AI detection, and ITS enterprise AI FAQ guidance for ChatGPT Edu/Codex and data handling. The claims remain agent-reviewed candidates only.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
Coursework claims are scoped to USC Office of Academic Integrity and ITS guidance; individual instructors may set course-specific rules.
The Provost ChatGPT Edu page was inaccessible during this run and was not used as evidence.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
